rag_rules: Repair only AGREE clauses with unresolved identifiers by aligning them to visible AADL features or declared eq helpers; do not invent new declarations.
Fix type mismatches by ensuring left and right sides of assignments and comparisons use compatible scalar types: int, real, or bool.
generic_rules: Prefer deletion of invalid clauses over architectural modification when identifiers are unresolved.
Use only ports, constants, or eq variables declared in the same annex as valid references in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE assignments (assign) are valid only in implementation annexes and must target output ports or declared auxiliary variables.
Scalar types in AGREE are strictly int, real, bool — never use AADL property names or unimported type aliases.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Never introduce package-qualified names (e.g., Agree_Nodes::Brake_Mode) not explicitly declared or imported in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
Implementation-level binding placement rules for component types
AGREE annex location semantics for assumptions and guarantees
Model fusion or owner scope preservation
